articles.title: Everything is gambling now: the latest news on prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi
articles.summary: Prediction markets are spreading beyond politics and finance into everything from weather forecasts to celebrity gossip, turning daily life into a series of tradable outcomes. This explosion of bet-able events creates massive new liquidity opportunities but also risks diluting serious predictive markets with noise and entertainment gambling.

articles.title: Ninth Circuit panel appears to lean Nevada’s way in legal battle with Kalshi, Crypto.com
articles.summary: A Ninth Circuit panel seems inclined to side with Nevada in its legal fight against prediction market Kalshi and crypto exchange Crypto.com. If Nevada wins, it could set a precedent that strengthens state gambling regulators' ability to challenge prediction markets operating without local licenses.

articles.title: Trump’s top regulator for prediction markets pledges crackdown on insider trading
articles.summary: Trump's incoming financial regulator plans to crack down on insider trading within prediction markets, targeting traders who exploit non-public information for profit. The move signals Washington will treat prediction markets more like traditional securities exchanges, potentially forcing platforms to adopt stricter monitoring and compliance measures that could reshape how political and economic events get priced.

articles.title: Vault: House Ag’s prediction market worries
articles.summary: The House Agriculture Committee is raising concerns about prediction markets, likely viewing them as a threat to traditional agricultural futures or worried about their regulatory oversight. If Congress starts targeting prediction markets with restrictive legislation, it could severely limit where Americans can legally bet on political outcomes and other events.

articles.title: A Polymarket trader made $300,000 betting on Biden's pardons, a new analysis shows
articles.summary: A trader on Polymarket scored $300,000 by correctly predicting which pardons President Biden would issue, according to new analysis of the platform's betting data. The windfall shows how political prediction markets are expanding beyond elections into granular executive decisions, creating fresh profit opportunities for bettors willing to track presidential patterns.

articles.title: The Newest Prediction Markets Guru Is a Middle Schooler in Braces
articles.summary: A middle school kid with braces is apparently crushing it on prediction markets and gaining a following as a forecasting expert. This matters because if a 13-year-old can outpredict adults with decades of experience, it either proves the markets are broken or that raw analytical thinking beats conventional wisdom.

articles.title: Kalshi's not having a good time in Ohio
articles.summary: Kalshi is facing regulatory pushback or legal troubles in Ohio that's disrupting their operations in the state. This signals growing scrutiny of prediction markets at the state level, which could create a patchwork of regulations that makes expansion harder for platforms trying to go mainstream.

articles.title: Rahm Emanuel proposes betting and prediction markets tax, defends CPS reforms as he mulls presidential bid
articles.summary: Rahm Emanuel is floating a betting and prediction markets tax while defending his Chicago Public Schools record as he considers jumping into the 2028 presidential race. A prediction markets tax from a potential Democratic frontrunner signals the party's growing appetite for regulating the space, especially after betting markets called Trump's victory before traditional polls.

articles.title: Kalshi Expands Commodity Predictions Market Amid War Volatility
articles.summary: Kalshi is launching new commodity prediction markets as geopolitical conflicts drive wild price swings in oil, wheat, and other raw materials. War-fueled volatility creates perfect conditions for traders to profit from commodity bets, giving Kalshi a fresh revenue stream while established players like CME face regulatory headwinds.

articles.title: 'Nothing Ever Happens': This Bot Always Bets 'No' on Polymarket, And It Has a Point
articles.summary: A bot on Polymarket automatically bets "No" on every prediction market, banking on the fact that most dramatic events people bet on simply don't materialize. The strategy highlights a fundamental bias in prediction markets where bettors consistently overestimate the likelihood of newsworthy outcomes, creating systematic profit opportunities for contrarians willing to bet against human nature.

articles.title: Kalshi Doubles Down on Commodities, Announces Market Expansion and Dedicated Hub
articles.summary: Kalshi is expanding its commodities trading offerings with new markets and a specialized trading hub. The move signals prediction markets are pushing beyond politics into traditional financial instruments, giving retail bettors access to commodity exposure that was previously limited to institutional traders.

articles.title: Prediction markets are a gamble we can't afford | Editorial
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets pose too much risk to society and should be restricted or banned. If this sentiment spreads among regulators, it could kill the momentum behind recently legalized political betting markets in the US before they really take off.
